title: Inequality in Évian
content: The G7 summit must recognize that extreme disparities in income, wealth, opportunity, and political influence are not only a defining feature of the global economy but also a major factor in many other shared challenges. For too long, the issue has been treated as a symptom rather than a cause.

creator: Adriana Abdenur
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: Europeans Are the G7’s Grownups
content: Slowly but surely, the European Union is taking steps to reduce its current-account surplus and direct more of its substantial savings into domestic investment and growth. Unfortunately, at the G7 Summit in France this month, the United States will refuse to address global imbalances, including its own.

creator: Laura Tyson
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: Modi Is Rigging Indian Democracy
content: Under the guise of routine voter-roll maintenance, India’s Election Commission has purged tens of millions of voters, disproportionately affecting opposition strongholds, the poor, and Muslim minorities. The result is an electoral system increasingly designed to favor the ruling Bharatiya Janata Party.
snippet: Under the guise of routine voter-roll maintenance, India’s Election Commission has purged tens of millions of voters, disproportionately affecting opposition strongholds, the poor, and Muslim
creator: Jayati Ghosh
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: Learning to Govern a Fragmented World
content: As the postwar multilateral order gives way to a multipolar and more contested world, global governance must adapt or risk irrelevance. The upcoming G7 summit offers a unique opportunity to champion a coalition-based approach that translates shared interests into collective action.
snippet: As the postwar multilateral order gives way to a multipolar and more contested world, global governance must adapt or risk irrelevance. The upcoming G7 summit offers a unique opportunity to champion a
creator: Dennis J. Snower
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: Cognition for Sale
content: AI is doing what no previous technology could, turning tacit human knowledge into a product that can be extracted, priced, and distributed at scale. Yet the same technology that democratizes cognitive labor may also weaken the human capacities on which complex societies depend.
snippet: AI is doing what no previous technology could, turning tacit human knowledge into a product that can be extracted, priced, and distributed at scale. Yet the same technology that democratizes cognitive
creator: Sami Mahroum
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: Big Tech Gets Bigger
content: Recent stock-market gyrations are a reminder of just how dependent the United States and global economies have become on investors’ seemingly insatiable appetite for tech stocks. Even if a major correction is in the offing, however, it will not happen before SpaceX’s massive initial public offering draws in yet more leveraged investment, nor will it destroy the market power of the other tech giants that have spent years perfecting anti-competitive tactics that largely escape regulators’ purview.
snippet: Recent stock-market gyrations are a reminder of just how dependent the United States and global economies have become on investors’ seemingly insatiable appetite for tech stocks. Even if a major
creator: PS Commentators
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: The Big Picture
creator: PS editors
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: The Democrats Need a Story, Not More Lists
content: Whereas Donald Trump has offered voters a clear and compelling narrative, Democrats have provided only vague aspirations and long lists of policies. To win over voters—rather than scrounging protest votes—they must articulate a compelling economic vision, with wage-led growth as its foundation.
snippet: Whereas Donald Trump has offered voters a clear and compelling narrative, Democrats have provided only vague aspirations and long lists of policies. To win over voters—rather than scrounging protest
creator: Teresa Ghilarducci
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: What if Europe Loses Access to Its Climate Data?
content: The European Green Deal runs on data, from corporate disclosures to climate-risk analyses and decarbonization pathways. But much of this data feeds platforms and tools developed outside the European Union, which could threaten the bloc’s climate agenda and even its foundational values.
snippet: The European Green Deal runs on data, from corporate disclosures to climate-risk analyses and decarbonization pathways. But much of this data feeds platforms and tools developed outside the European
creator: Bertrand Badré
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: Raising Interest Rates Won’t Stabilize African Inflation
content: Monetary tightening in Africa would not only fail to rein in inflation; it would raise the cost of productive investment, suppress growth, and hamper the continent's much-needed structural transformation. A better strategy would focus on accelerating this transformation by channeling finance into productive industries.
snippet: Monetary tightening in Africa would not only fail to rein in inflation; it would raise the cost of productive investment, suppress growth, and hamper the continent's much-needed structural
creator: Célestin Monga
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: The Enduring Enigma of Economic Growth
content: Developing countries have made enormous progress in many of the factors behind growth, reflected in stronger institutions, rising education levels, higher life expectancy, and increased investment rates. Yet income convergence with advanced economies has been painfully slow—and in most cases absent.
snippet: Developing countries have made enormous progress in many of the factors behind growth, reflected in stronger institutions, rising education levels, higher life expectancy, and increased investment
creator: Andrés Velasco
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: Is the US Stock Market Too Big?
content: With China and India having grown so fast as to compensate for the underperformance of their fellow BRICS (Brazil, Russia, and South Africa), the center of gravity in the global economy continues to shift eastward. The question now is whether—and when—equity valuations will reflect this underlying reality.
snippet: With China and India having grown so fast as to compensate for the underperformance of their fellow BRICS (Brazil, Russia, and South Africa), the center of gravity in the global economy continues to
creator: Jim O'Neill
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: Brazil’s Bad Vibes
content: A government that abandons structural agendas whenever sentiment turns negative will still be managing sentiment four years later—with nothing to show for it. Brazil’s “vibecession” is a real problem for the current government; but winning the news cycle is not a real solution.
snippet: A government that abandons structural agendas whenever sentiment turns negative will still be managing sentiment four years later—with nothing to show for it. Brazil’s “vibecession” is a real problem
creator: Laura Carvalho
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: Negotiating With the Antichrist
content: Apocalyptic thinking is seeping into the political discourse, with figures like Peter Thiel, Pete Hegseth, and Aleksandr Dugin portraying political differences as struggles between good and evil. When rivals become mortal enemies, the instruments of politics and diplomacy cannot work.
snippet: Apocalyptic thinking is seeping into the political discourse, with figures like Peter Thiel, Pete Hegseth, and Aleksandr Dugin portraying political differences as struggles between good and evil. When
creator: Nina L. Khrushcheva
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: Why Is Europe’s Economy Falling Short?
content: For much of the postwar era, European policymakers have prioritized stability and predictability over fostering breakthrough innovation. Today, as the United States and China compete for AI dominance, a stagnant Europe is struggling to regain its place at the technological frontier.
snippet: For much of the postwar era, European policymakers have prioritized stability and predictability over fostering breakthrough innovation. Today, as the United States and China compete for AI dominance,
creator: Philippe Aghion
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: Big Tech’s Shadow Market Power
content: Big Tech firms are amassing market power through strategies that bypass current rules for oversight, investigation, and intervention. Unless and until regulators account for the full scope and scale of these strategies, Silicon Valley’s dominance will remain unchallenged.
snippet: Big Tech firms are amassing market power through strategies that bypass current rules for oversight, investigation, and intervention. Unless and until regulators account for the full scope and scale
creator: Helena Malikova
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: The AI Revolution Mirrors the Green Transition
content: The need for massive upfront investments and the likelihood of significant job displacement imply remarkable parallels between the AI buildout and the green transition. In both cases, the state has an important role to play in guiding market forces on behalf of the public good.
snippet: The need for massive upfront investments and the likelihood of significant job displacement imply remarkable parallels between the AI buildout and the green transition. In both cases, the state has an
creator: Adam Michael Bauer
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: Where Is China’s Trillion-Dollar Trade Surplus Going?
content: Whereas China’s trade surplus used to be absorbed largely by the central bank and channeled into official reserves, it is now being redeployed by private actors in search of yield. This indicates China’s progress on financial deepening and underscores markets' increasingly central role in shaping the economy.

creator: Miao Yanliang
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: The Future of Development Finance Is Not Primarily About Money
content: Multilateral development banks have not kept pace with changes to the global financial environment. For middle-income countries like India, accessing knowledge and technology is now a bigger challenge than raising capital, which suggests that these institutions must rethink their operating model.
snippet: Multilateral development banks have not kept pace with changes to the global financial environment. For middle-income countries like India, accessing knowledge and technology is now a bigger challenge
creator: Tanu M. Goyal
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: Peak Oil Demand Has Arrived
content: Amid the disruptions caused by the US/Israeli-Iran war, countries have begun adapting to life with less oil. What started as a supply shock has become a demand story, forcing the energy sector to reconsider one of its most important assumptions: continued growth of global oil demand.
